prependContext:
<relevant-memories>
  The following memories are relevant to this query. Reference only if helpful:

  - [instruction|AI helping software developer with Date selection feature design] User requires AI to adhere strictly to external design documentation (e.g., agent.md and Design.md) for all UI/UX decisions. (skill: UI/UX Design)
  - [instruction|AI helping software engineer with UI/UX Design] User requires AI to always ensure new components (like the Date Duo Spinner) adhere visually and structurally to established modal patterns defined in adjacent project files, and must follow all design guidelines stipulated in 'Design.md'. (skill: UI/UX Design)
  - [episodic|AI helping software engineer with refining date planning app features] User formally approved the technical specification and implementation plan for the Drag-and-Drop Reordering feature, which required setting up a new PUT /reorder endpoint and integrating Framer Motion's <Reorder.Group> on the frontend. (skill: UI/UX Design)
  - [episodic|AI helping user with improving DateItinerary Collections feature] User initiated a phase of feature discovery by asking to read existing documentation (AGENT.md) and brainstorming missing features specifically within the collections/duo context. (skill: UI/UX Design)
  - [episodic|AI helping software engineer with refining date planning app features] User conducted a thorough QA sequence confirming core collaborative features: successful multi-user syncing (User A seeing "Syncing" and User B seeing "Accept/Reject"), and verifying the presence of a "Match!" badge across the Explore list and Spot Detail page. (skill: UI/UX Design)
</relevant-memories>

recalledL1Memories.content: User requires AI to adhere strictly to external design documentation (e.g., agent.md and Design.md) for all UI/UX decisions.

User requires AI to always ensure new components (like the Date Duo Spinner) adhere visually and structurally to established modal patterns defined in adjacent project files, and must follow all design guidelines stipulated in 'Design.md'.

User formally approved the technical specification and implementation plan for the Drag-and-Drop Reordering feature, which required setting up a new PUT /reorder endpoint and integrating Framer Motion's <Reorder.Group> on the frontend.
User initiated a phase of feature discovery by asking to read existing documentation (AGENT.md) and brainstorming missing features specifically within the collections/duo context.

User conducted a thorough QA sequence confirming core collaborative features: successful multi-user syncing (User A seeing "Syncing" and User B seeing "Accept/Reject"), and verifying the presence of a "Match!" badge across the Explore list and Spot Detail page.
